title: LTEE Media Recipes
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"44f0p","text":" Growth media used by the long-term E. coli evolution experiment.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":36,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fsqfc","text":"TA: Tetrazolium Arabinose for distinguishing Ara- and Ara+ strains in most competition assays. Colonies of Ara- strains typically appear red on TA agar, while those of Ara+ strains appear white. TA plates are generally incubated at 37°C for 24 h.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b1pf","text":"DM: The basic medium used for propagating the long-term lines is Davis Minimal broth supplemented with glucose at a concentration of 25 mg per L, which we refer to as DM25. This medium supports a stationary-phase density of about 5 x 107 cells per ml for the founding strain of E. coli B.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":236,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7cp8e","text":"MG: Same basic composition as for DM liquid medium, except that we: add agar (as solidifying agent), increase the sugar concentration (so that colonies are robust), and sometimes use arabinose (instead of glucose).  Glucose is used to examine the colonies on the standard minimal medium.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: To prepare 1 L of DM:
Add distilled water to a final volume of 1 L
To prepare 1.5 L of TA:

Autoclave using liquid program. Sterilization times are based on total volume: Volume (ml) Time (min) 1 75-200 20 2 200-500 25 3 500-1000 30 4 1000-1500 35 5 1500-2000 40 6 >2000 60 Sterilization time

After autoclaving add the following stock solutions: a. 1 mL of previously sterilized Magnesium Sulfate P212121 at 10 Mass / % volume s b. 1 mL of filtered sterilized Thiamine HCl P212121 at 0.2 Mass / % volume

If preparing DM-glucose, add this volume of Glucose P212121 Catalog #Glucose (separately autoclaved stock) at 10 Mass / % volume , to get the final concentration desired: %s %s 125 µl 250 µl 1.25 ml DM25 0.0025% 25 mg/L 139 µM 0.5 ml 1 ml 5 ml DM100 0.010% 100 mg/L 694 µM 1.25 ml 2.5 ml 12.5 ml DM250 0.025% 250 mg/L 1.39 µM 2.5 ml 5 ml 25 ml DM500 0.05% 500 mg/L 2.78 mM 5 ml 10 ml 50 ml DM1000 0.1% 1000 mg/L 5.55 mM 10 ml 20 ml 100 ml DM2000 0.2% 2000 mg/L 11.1 mM Remember: DMX = DM + X mg/L glucose. Glucose may no longer limit the final growth density above approximately DM1000. Remember: DMX = DM + X mg/L glucose. Final composition: Sodium (Na + ) = 5.1 millimolar (mM) Potassium (K + ) = 75.8 millimolar (mM) Ammonium (NH 4 ) = 15.2 millimolar (mM) Magnesium (Mg 2+ ) = 0.83 millimolar (mM) Sulfate (SO 4 2- ) = 8.41 millimolar (mM) Phosphate (PO 4 3- ) = 45.3 millimolar (mM) Citrate = 1.7 millimolar (mM) (In DM25) Glucose = 139 micromolar (µM)
Add distilled water to 1.3 L

Separately, prepare sugar solution by combining: a. 15 g of L-( )-Arabinose Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #A3256-500G (CAS: 5328-37-0) b. 200 mL of distilled water Sugar could be substituted for any other sugar.
Autoclave both solutions, media base and sugar solution from go to step #2 and go to step #2.3 separatley and according to sterilization table in go to step #1.3 .
Combine sterile solutions, media base and sugar solution for a total of 1.5 L
Add 1.5 mL of (filter sterilized and stored at 4 °C ) 235-Triphenyltetrazolium chloride Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #T8877 (CAS: 298-96-4) at 5 Mass / % volume

When making these plates it is necessary to prepare and autoclave the 3 main parts (salt solution, agar base, and sugar solution) separately . Compounds that inhibit growth are produced when agar and phosphate or phosphate and glucose are autoclaved together.

Prepare salt solution , combine: a. 5.3 g of Potassium phosphate (dibasic) P212121 b. 2 g of Potassium phosphate (monobasic) P212121 c. 1 g of Ammonium sulfate Contributed by users Catalog #97061-184 d. 0.5 g of Trisodium citrate dihydrate Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #S1804 e. 400 mL of distilled water

Prepare agar base by combining: a. 16 g of Agar Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #A1296 (CAS: 9002-18-0) b. 1 mL of Antifoam B Emulsion Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #A5757-250ML c. 400 mL of distilled water

Weigh dry components: a. 5.34 g of Potassium phosphate (dibasic) P212121 or 7 g of Potassium phosphate dibasic trihydrate Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #P9666 (CAS: 16788-57-1) b. 2 g of Potassium phosphate (monobasic) P212121 c. 1 g of Ammonium sulfate Contributed by users Catalog #97061-184 d. 0.5 g of Trisodium citrate dihydrate Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #S1804

Prepare media base by combining in a 2L flask: a. 15 g of Bacto™ Tryptone Thermo Fisher Catalog #211705 b. 1.5 g of Bacto™ Yeast Extract Thermo Fisher Catalog #212750 c. 7.5 g of Sodium Chloride Fisher Scientific Catalog # MK-7581-212 (CAS: 7647-14-5) d. 24 g of Agar Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #A1296 (CAS: 9002-18-0) e. 1.5 mL of Antifoam B Emulsion Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #A5757-250ML
To prepare 1 L of MG:
Prepare sugar solution by combining: a. 4 g of Glucose P212121 Catalog #Glucose b. 200 mL of distilled water Sugar could be substituted for any other sugar.
Autoclave sugar solution according to go to step #1.3
Autoclave salt solution according to go to step #1.3
Autoclave agar base according to go to step #1.3

After the three parts have been autoclaved, combine the contents of the three flasks together while they are still warm add the following stock solutions: a. 1 mL of Magnesium Sulfate P212121 at 10 Mass / % volume (separately autoclaved stock) b. 1 mL of Thiamine HCl P212121 at 0.2 Mass / % volume (filter sterilized stock)
creator: Jeffrey E Barrick
